headline: Here Are Friday’s Top Wall Street Analyst Research Calls: BorgWarner, CoreWeave, Intuit, Marvell Technology, Netflix, Okta, Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Trade Desk, and More
summary: Pre-Market Stock Futures: Futures are trading lower as we prepare to finally end what has been one of the most volatile weeks in recent memory, with oil hitting its highest level since 2024. After a snap-back Wednesday that saw all the major indices close higher, the tables were turned on investors once again as oil ... headline: The AES Corporation (AES) Price Target Cut to $23 at Morgan Stanley
summary: The AES Corporation (NYSE:AES) is among the 11 Most Undervalued Utility Stocks to Buy Now. On February 20, Morgan Stanley lowered the firm’s price target on The AES Corporation (NYSE:AES) to $23 from $24 while maintaining an Overweight rating. The adjustment reflects broader updates to price targets across Regulated & Diversified Utilities and Independent Power […]

headline: This Trade Desk Analyst Turns Bearish; Here Are Top 5 Downgrades For Friday
summary: Top Wall Street analysts change outlook on top names. See all rating changes on Benzinga's analyst ratings page. Analysts have mixed views on TTD stock.

headline: Okta To Rally Around 22%? Here Are 10 Top Analyst Forecasts For Friday
summary: Top Wall Street analysts changed their outlook on these top names. For a complete view of all analyst rating changes, including upgrades and downgrades, visit our analyst ratings page. B of A Securities raised Marvell Technology Inc price target from $90 to $110. Keefe, Bruyette & Woods cut Hippo Holdings Inc price target from $34 to $33. Piper Sandler raised Karman Holdings Inc price target from $110 to $127. Morgan Stanley cut AES Corp price target from $23 to $15. Clear Street raised Erasca Inc price target from $11 to $20. Baird raised Union Pacific Corp price target from $239 to $311. BTIG increased Costco Wholesale Corp price target from $1,115 to $1,125. B. Riley Securities slashed Brilliant Earth Group Inc price target from $29 to $25.

headline: AES: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
summary: The Arlington, Virginia-based company said it had profit of 45 cents per share. Earnings, adjusted for non-recurring costs, were 81 cents per share. The results exceeded Wall Street expectations.

headline: Morgan Stanley Downgrades AES to Equal-Weight, Lowers Price Target to $15
summary: Morgan Stanley  analyst Stephen Byrd   downgrades AES (NYSE:AES) from Overweight to Equal-Weight and lowers the price target from $23 to $15.
